RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT IN HOME SUPPORT SERVICES, CONSORTIUM Motion To File Under Seal IS DENIED, C R C 20550 AND 2.551 REQUIRE THAT A MOTION TO SEAL DOCUMENTS BE BROUGHT BEFORE THEY HAVE BEEN FILED. Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim
Off Calendar Feb-15-2007 Continued To Mar-08-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Compromise of an Adult Person with a Disablity Hearing and for an Order authorizing establishment of Pooled Special Needs Trust Subaccount Hearing this day.
Dept. 218, Comm. Sue Kaplan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: C. Sauceda.
Michael Fox, Esq. for Petitioner: James U. Hodges, present as guardian ad litem for Diable Adult Claimant: Ellen Gutierrez.
Rebecca Paul: Esq. for Disable Adult Claimant: Ellen Gutierrez.
Christopher Beeman, Esq., Edward Koplowitz, Esq., from IHSS, are also present in court.
Mr. Hodges was sworn and direct examined.
After hearing the testimony and evidence presented, the Court continued the matter to Mar. 8, 2007 at 9:00 A.M. in Dept. 218.
Counsel to re-service the government entities with proper Notice of Hearing on the Special Needs Trust and file Proof of Service.

Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-19-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Elizabeth Ryan, Esq., for On Lok, Inc., Defendant/Moving Party
Rebecca R. Paul, Esq., for Ellen Gutierrez, Plaintiff/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant On Lok, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form And Special Interrogatories is granted. Interrogatories are proper as they seek a party's contentions. A party is required to to state their contentions and the facts upon which those contentions are based. [See Shooker v. Superior Court (2003) 111 Cal.App.4th 923.] COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
